channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S. Olympic speed skaters adapt NASCAR 'bump drafting,' revolutionizing team event
item.description: U.S. Team Pursuit speed skaters will top speeds of 30 mph by pushing themselves around the track mere inches from each other.

item.title: How to Host an Unforgettable Dumpling Party
item.description: Four expert tips turn prep work into a major part of the fun.

item.title: Why an A.I. Video of Tom Cruise Battling Brad Pitt Spooked Hollywood
item.description: A 15-second clip created by an artificial intelligence tool owned by the Chinese technology company ByteDance appears more cinematic than anything so ...

item.title: 5 New Dumpling Recipes for Lunar New Year 2026
item.description: It’s Dumpling Week, our annual tribute to one of everyone’s favorite foods, with recipes to get you pinching and pleating.

item.title: From One President to Another, a Love Letter With an Edge
item.description: To open a series of essays about U.S. presidents, George W. Bush pays tribute to George Washington, who “ensured America wouldn’t become a monarchy, o...

item.title: 'American Struggle' author assesses Trump's expansion of presidential power
item.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Jon Meacham talks about Trump's impact on democracy. Meacham's latest book is a collection of speeches, letters and o...

item.title: Israel will begin contentious West Bank land registration
item.description: Israel will begin a contentious land regulation process in a large part of the occupied West Bank, which could result in Israel gaining control over w...

item.title: How to register to vote in the 2026 primaries
item.description: For the 2026 primary elections, NPR has collected deadlines and information on how to register to vote — online, in person or by mail — in every U.S. ...

item.title: Is that carb ultra-processed? Here's a test even a kid can do
item.description: The latest nutrition guidelines urge Americans to avoid highly-processed food. But, when it comes to carbs, many people don't know which ones are ultr...

item.title: With Latest Rollback, the U.S. Essentially Has No Clean-Car Rules
item.description: The E.P.A.’s killing of the “endangerment finding” caps a year of deregulation that is likely to make cars thirstier for gas and less competitive glob...

item.title: Iran’s Protests Have Ended, but the Anger and Pain Haven’t
item.description: In the aftermath of another wave of antigovernment unrest, Iran is gripped by a mood of collective grief and uncertainty about the future.

item.title: Syrian Army Crosses New Threshold With Advances in Kurdish Regions
item.description: The advance of the Syrian Army into Kurdish regions could put nearly all the country under one authority. But it ends a dream of autonomy for ethnic K...

item.title: Cortina’s 70-Year-Old Curling Stadium Is a Star at the Winter Olympics
item.description: It hosted the 1956 Olympics and had a role in a James Bond movie. Now Cortina’s beloved stadium is packed with raucous fans, and the occasional cheati...

item.title: Prices Jump as Venezuelans Abroad Consider Buying Property Back Home
item.description: Nicolás Maduro’s capture and talk of oil investment have pushed prices higher, even as brokers say enthusiasm is outpacing demand in a weak economy.

item.title: A Russian ‘Village of Military Valor’ Waits for Its Reward
item.description: A provincial governor seeks to honor villages that deployed many soldiers to the war in Ukraine with a prestigious title first bestowed on cities that...
